title: Calomecon bracteatum
taxonomy: Calomecon bracteatum is classified within the family Asclepiadaceae, which is now considered a synonym for the family Apocynaceae. However, some sources still recognize Asclepiadaceae as a separate family. Further classification places Calomecon bracteatum within the subfamily Periplocoideae of Apocynaceae. This subfamily is characterized by its unique floral and seed capsule characteristics. The specific classification of Calomecon bracteatum may vary depending on the source and taxonomic system used.

intro: Calomecon bracteatum, also known as Mexican Buckthorn or Mexican Buckeye, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its compound leaves and showy white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
